attributes.description: Alerts when free storage space on an RDS instance falls below an absolute byte floor. Storage exhaustion is an outage-class risk; total volume size is not published by CloudWatch so percentage thresholds cannot be derived from this source.
attributes.name: [AWS RDS OTel] Free storage space low

investigation_guide.blob:
## Free Storage Space Low

### Triage
1. Confirm the instance and region from the alert context.
2. Check storage autoscaling settings and current allocated storage in the AWS RDS console.
3. Review recent growth trends — large imports, index builds, or log table bloat are common causes.
4. Inspect `BinLogDiskUsage` if binlog/WAL volume may be consuming space.

### Mitigation
- Enable or increase storage autoscaling.
- Archive or purge old data; shrink large tables or indexes.
- Increase allocated storage before the volume fills completely.

### Note
Adjust the byte floor in the rule query to match your smallest instance volumes and growth rate.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-aws.rds.otel-*
| WHERE Namespace == "AWS/RDS"
    AND MetricName == "FreeStorageSpace"
    AND stat == "Average"
| STATS min_free_storage = MIN(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/FreeStorageSpace`)
    BY DBInstanceIdentifier, cloud.region
// Absolute byte floor — adjust to your volume sizes (default: 10 GB)
| WHERE min_free_storage < 10737418240
| SORT min_free_storage ASC
